items.title: This postseason, Spurs' 22-and-under players have produced 1,325 points. That is the most such points by any team in a single playoff run in NBA history.
items.author: NBA Stat
items.body_excerpt:
This postseason, Spurs' 22-and-under players have produced 1,325 points. That is the most such points by any team in a single playoff run in NBA history.

2026 Spurs - 1,325 points
2011 Thunder - 1,280
2021 Hawks - 765
2018 Celtics - 679
2007 Cavaliers - 666

(via CrazyStats)
#NBA

items.title: From June 19, 1865 to today — Juneteenth carries the story of freedom realized and still pursued. Ms. Opal Lee helped ensure that story is never forgotten. The NBA Family honors her leadership and the timeless importance of freedom, family, and tradition reflected in this day.

items.title: June 19, 1994: Hakeem Olajuwon blocks John Starks at the buzzer in Game 6 of the NBA Finals, and Houston defeats New York, 86-84—to force a Game 7.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 19, 1994: Hakeem Olajuwon blocks John Starks at the buzzer in Game 6 of the NBA Finals, and Houston defeats New York, 86-84—to force a Game 7.

Olajuwon had 30 PTS/10 REB/4 BLK and Carl Herrera scored 12 PTS for the Rockets. Starks had 27 PTS (5-9 3P)/8 AST for the Knicks.

items.author: Steph Noh
items.body_excerpt:

-Dad played 6 NFL seasons
-Mom played 3 high school sports
-Six older siblings all DI athletes
-Brother Andrus 3x NFL Pro Bowler

On family competitions, his football skills, and why he thinks he has some Paolo/Kawhi in his game: 

www.sportingnews.com/us/nba/news/...
